explanation: The requirement states that the self-test procedure must confirm that the switchover from external to internal power functions correctly. Given the atomic proposition 'testPowerSwitchPass' means this confirmation was successful, a reasonable translation is to specify that it must eventually be true during the self-test (i.e., testPowerSwitchPass must happen at some point). To keep this general, we use the globally event "finally" property so that in every execution, at some point, the test passes. Thus we select template 9 ('F bool_exp3') and set bool_exp3 to 'testPowerSwitchPass'. N_DURATION is not needed since template 9 is unbounded.

The requirement specifies that the self-test procedure must confirm that the switchover from external to internal power works. In the context of the given atomic propositions, 'testPowerSwitchPass' directly indicates a successful test confirming the switchover works. Therefore, the appropriate LTL property is that globally (at all times), it must be the case that the test for confirming the switchover from external to internal power is a success. This matches the requirement that the self-test shall confirm this switchover, effectively requiring that 'testPowerSwitchPass' must always hold true at the relevant point(s) in the self-test. The most general formalization is simply that G(testPowerSwitchPass), stipulating that this property must always be satisfied (including during every self-test procedure).

The natural language requirement states that the self-test procedure must confirm the system's ability to switch from external to internal power. The atomic proposition 'testPowerSwitchPass' directly indicates this successful confirmation. To capture the requirement, we express that globally (i.e., at all times), whenever the self-test is supposed to confirm the switchover works, the atomic proposition 'testPowerSwitchPass' should hold. Since the requirement simply asks that the confirmation (testPowerSwitchPass) occurs (without a conditional or time constraint in the statement), the best matching template is the 'eventually' operator F(testPowerSwitchPass), ensuring that at some point, the confirmation occurs. No other conditions are part of the statement, so no need for more complex templates.

The input sentence states that the self-test procedure should confirm that the switchover from external to internal power works. In the atomic propositions, 'testPowerSwitchPass' refers to the success of the switchover functionality test, and 'selfTestPass' refers to all self-test steps passing successfully. The natural language requirement implies that a successful self-test must include verifying that the power switchover works. Thus, whenever selfTestPass occurs, testPowerSwitchPass must have occurred. This is best captured using a general implication: globally, if selfTestPass holds, then testPowerSwitchPass must also hold, i.e., G (selfTestPass -> testPowerSwitchPass). This guarantees that the self-test passing implies the power switchover test also passes.

The requirement is for the self-test procedure to confirm that switchover from external to internal power works. This means that as part of the self-test (represented by completion event selfTestPass), the system must include a passing test that confirms the power switchover feature. The most direct atomic proposition reflecting this is 'testPowerSwitchPass'. Therefore, the LTL property should state that globally, whenever the self-test completes successfully (selfTestPass), it must be the case that the switchover test passes (testPowerSwitchPass). Thus, we use the template that states globally, whenever one condition (selfTestPass) holds, a second (testPowerSwitchPass) must also hold (implication): G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3).

The requirement states that the self-test must confirm the switchover from external to internal power works. Among the atomic propositions, 'testPowerSwitchPass' specifically denotes success of the test for the power switchover. A successful self-test should always ensure this test passes. Therefore, we capture this as: 'Globally, if the self-test passes, it implies the power switchover test passes', corresponding to LTL form G (selfTestPass -> testPowerSwitchPass). This ensures that whenever the self-test is declared to have passed, it truly confirmed the functionality of the switchover.

The natural language requirement is that the self-test procedure should confirm that the switchover from external to internal power works. The most direct mapping in the provided atomic propositions is 'testPowerSwitchPass', which indicates a successful test confirming the functionality of the power switchover. Since this is a self-test check that must pass, the most likely LTL property is an invariant (globally) stating that testPowerSwitchPass must always hold (unless more detail is specified about conditions or sequence, which the prompt does not supply). This ensures that any execution always confirms the switchover mechanism works, satisfying the requirement.

The natural language input specifies that the self-test must confirm the power switch switchover from external to internal power works. Among the atomic propositions, 'testPowerSwitchPass' directly captures the successful confirmation of the switchover. Therefore, this requirement can be suitably formalized as 'eventually the testPowerSwitchPass condition becomes true', i.e., the system eventually confirms the switchover fulfils the requirement. Thus, the most appropriate template is 'F bool_exp3' (template 9), with bool_exp3 = testPowerSwitchPass and an unbounded eventuality to ensure that the system at some point confirms the functionality, as required.

The requirement states that the self-test procedure must confirm the switchover from external to internal power works. Among available atomic propositions, 'testPowerSwitchPass' directly maps to the successful completion of a switchover test. The LTL property should capture that at some point (eventually) during the procedure, this confirmation ('testPowerSwitchPass') occurs. The most direct LTL template for this requirement is 'eventually within a bounded duration, testPowerSwitchPass is true', which is translated to F[0,N_DURATION] testPowerSwitchPass. This asserts that within a fixed time window (set here as N_DURATION=5), the confirmation will happen, adequately capturing the natural language requirement for a timely self-test switchover confirmation.

The requirement states that the self-test procedure should confirm that the switchover from external to internal power works. Among the atomic propositions, 'testPowerSwitchPass' is defined as indicating a successful confirmation of the switchover, which aligns directly with the requirement. Thus, the LTL property G(testPowerSwitchPass) specifies that at all times (e.g., during self-test), this confirmation holds, but more commonly in such requirements it is interpreted that this must eventually be confirmed during the self-test procedure. However, since there is no explicit 'self-test' procedure atomic proposition in the mapping, we use 'testPowerSwitchPass' directly as the key indicator of the desired confirmation. Therefore, the global (always) property is the most direct translation.
